name: Melbourne After Dark
shortDescription: Five works. One city. A light that never quite arrives.
fullDescription: Melbourne rendered in shadow and red — Luna Park's great mouth open onto something older than fun, Flinders Street Station flooded and watched over by a cloaked figure, a dragon coiling behind the CBD skyline under a blood moon. Each work begins as an original photograph taken on location. Every stage from there is handled personally — digital and physical work, colour, composition, editing — moving between techniques as each piece demands. The result is art that could only have been made by someone who was actually there. Linocut, woodcut and dark oil-painting styles — each piece lives in the tension between the city you recognise and the one that watches back. Red, black, and bone white. Familiar. Unsettling. Unmistakably Melbourne. Suits interiors that want presence — dark timbers, deep tones, spaces that don't apologise for themselves.

name: St Kilda Vibes
shortDescription: Eight works. One suburb. Every version of it at once.
fullDescription: St Kilda has never been just one thing — and this collection doesn't pretend otherwise. Luna Park grins from the dark like it always has. The Number 16 rounds the corner past the Palais under a sky that hasn't decided if it's burning or blooming. Catani Gardens at sunset. The foreshore rendered in Japanese woodblock ink, a white bird cutting across a blood moon. The Esplanade Hotel holding its ground against a swirling red sky like it has for a hundred years. Each work begins as an original photograph taken on location in St Kilda. Every stage from there is handled personally — digital and physical work, colour, composition, editing — moving between techniques as each piece demands. Eight pieces. The beach suburb, the gothic suburb, the glamorous suburb, the strange suburb — all of them true, all of them St Kilda. Suits coastal homes, Fitzroy Street apartments, and anything within earshot of the sea.

name: Tāonga / The Living Land
shortDescription: Seven works. One inheritance. A country that never stopped breathing.
fullDescription: Koru spirals holding entire coastlines inside their curl. Pou carved from something older than memory. Tūī cutting across feathers woven in gold, earth and ocean teal. Each work moves between the sacred and the elemental — flax and silver fern, waka on golden water, the New Zealand flag carried quietly in the tip of a feather like a declaration that needs no audience. Each piece begins as an original photograph taken on location. Every stage from there is handled personally — digital and physical work, colour, composition, editing — moving between techniques as each piece demands. Rich in ochre, forest green and deep Pacific blue, these works sit between illustration and ceremonial object. At home in any space that wants to feel both grounded and alive — particularly suited to new builds, light-filled interiors and homes where the land outside the window still means something.
